title: leucanthemum vulgare var. laciniatum
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare var. laciniatum, commonly known as the cutleaf daisy or fern-leafed daisy, is a variety of the oxeye daisy plant characterized by its fern-like foliage and white daisy-like flowers. Traditionally, Leucanthemum vulgare var. laciniatum has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. The medicinal properties of this plant are said to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Leucanthemum vulgare var. laciniatum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with medications and allergies.
